source_cross_references.note: runtime_context_requirements declares: context_type: medication / key: hormone_therapy / requirement: present AND context_type: clinical_context / key: aas_exposure / requirement: present. Verified as representative of all 8 androgen packages.

snapshot['medication']['hormone_therapy'] is set to True ONLY when the medication list contains 'testosterone' or 'hormone' keywords. A patient not on hormone therapy will have no 'hormone_therapy' key in the snapshot. The evaluator will treat this as missing required context and suppress the signal — even when the patient has fully disclosed their medication history.

snapshot['clinical_context']['aas_exposure'] is set to True ONLY when supplements contain 'steroid' or 'prohormone'. A patient with no AAS exposure will have no 'aas_exposure' key — signal suppressed even if disclosure is complete and AAS non-exposure is the relevant clinical context.

Any requirement type other than 'present' or 'lab_range_boundary' falls through to missing.append(). If Cursor encodes 'disclosed' or any new type in package YAML without updating the evaluator, the requirement will ALWAYS be unsatisfied — signal permanently suppressed. See STOP C.

All 8 androgen packages: activated: false, final_state: compiled_not_promoted_inactive or never_promoted_inactive. FT3 low at lines 43-57: activated: false, final_state: compiled_not_promoted_inactive.

CF-BATCH2-010 Open: 'No clinical sign-off artefact in repo.' This means all 8 androgen packages must receive BLOCKED_PENDING_CLINICAL_SIGNOFF or BLOCKED_PENDING_CONTEXT_SEMANTICS clearance decisions. CLEARED_FOR_STOP_GATED_ACTIVATION is not available to any androgen package in this sprint.

mandatory_stop_conditions_for_cursor.STOP_A: Duplicate Authority — If the proposed runtime_context_semantics_model_v1.yaml contains runtime_consumed: true, OR redefines supported_context_types / evaluator_path / runtime_contract fields that exist in runtime_context_requirements_model_v1.yaml, STOP before committing and report. The new file must reference the existing model as authority (e.g., semantic_authority_for: runtime_context_requirements_model_v1.yaml) and extend it with semantic type definitions only.
mandatory_stop_conditions_for_cursor.STOP_B: Runtime / Evaluator Changes — If at any point implementation determines that runtime_context_evaluator.py, signal_evaluator.py, orchestrator.py, or any other runtime code file must be changed in this sprint, STOP and report before making any changes. Those changes belong in BATCH2-CONTEXT-ACTIVATION-1.
mandatory_stop_conditions_for_cursor.STOP_C: Package YAML New Requirement Types — If Cursor edits any signal_library.yaml to change requirement: present to requirement: disclosed or any other new type, STOP immediately. The evaluator at runtime_context_evaluator.py:312 falls through unknown types to missing.append() — any new type will always evaluate as unsatisfied and permanently suppress the signal. Package semantic corrections require a separate sprint with evaluator changes included and regression-tested.

hardening_notes: Sprint is hardened and may proceed to Stage 3. Three non-negotiable STOP conditions apply (STOP_A: duplicate authority, STOP_B: runtime code changes, STOP_C: package YAML new requirement types). Key facts for Cursor: (1) The semantic ambiguity is confirmed — hormone_therapy and aas_exposure in signal_library.yaml requirement: present means 'patient has the condition' not 'patient has disclosed'; this is a false-negative risk that this sprint documents in governance artefacts. (2) This sprint's outputs are governance-only — no runtime code changes, no package activation, no evaluator changes. (3) All 8 androgen packages and FT3 low remain inactive and must remain so after this sprint. (4) CF-BATCH2-010 remains Open — no clearance decision can be CLEARED_FOR_STOP_GATED_ACTIVATION without a clinical sign-off artefact. (5) backend/tests/governance/ does not exist — create it. (6) The batch2_remaining_blockers_execution_register is stale on CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 — acknowledge in audit paper, do not amend that register. HIGH risk route: Cursor implementation → Claude audit → GPT architectural review → human approval before merge.
